paper_id: Calhau and de Almeida Falbo - 2012 - A Configuration Management task ontology for semantic integration

evidence_quotes.quote: This ontology was built with the purpose of supporting semantic integration of CM systems, mainly in service and process layers of integration.
The proposed ontology was used in a semantic integration effort aiming at integrating Subversion (SVN) [14] and CM-ODE.
These mappings guided the choice of which tools to use in each circumstance, and how to integrate them.

goals.justification: The ontology was actively used as a reference model to enable semantic integration between SVN and CM-ODE, with concrete mappings guiding tool integration — matching the For Intervening signal 'used to enable interoperability' and explicit use to integrate systems.

evidence_quotes.quote: The primary purpose of the proposed CM task ontology is to make the best possible description of the CM process, elaborating a model of consensus within this community.
to help semantic integration, ontologies can be used to establish a common conceptualization, explaining concepts and their meanings, and avoiding conflicts of understanding.
to achieve semantic integration, it is essential that the parties share a common understanding regarding the CM universe of discourse, including both its concepts and tasks.

goals.justification: The ontology is explicitly framed as a model of consensus aimed at shared conceptualization and clarifying meaning of CM concepts — matching the For Understanding signals 'conceptualize', 'shared understanding', and meaning negotiation.

evidence_quotes.quote: It is a solution-independent specification with the aim of making a clear and precise description of entities in this universe of discourse, for the purposes of communication, learning and problem-solving.

It is a solution-independent specification with the aim of making a clear and precise description of entities in the universe of discourse, for the purposes of communication, learning and problem-solving.

goals.justification: The paper explicitly states problem-solving as one of the purposes of the reference ontology, satisfying the For Problem-solving definition.

evidence_quotes.quote: It is a solution-independent specification with the aim of making a clear and precise description of entities in this universe of discourse, for the purposes of communication, learning and problem-solving.

It is a solution-independent specification with the aim of making a clear and precise description of entities in the universe of discourse, for the purposes of communication, learning and problem-solving.

goals.justification: The paper explicitly names communication as one of the ontology's purposes within a community of stakeholders, matching the For Communicating signal 'communicate' between distinct parties.

evidence_quotes.quote: It is a solution-independent specification with the aim of making a clear and precise description of entities in this universe of discourse, for the purposes of communication, learning and problem-solving.

uncertain_goals.reason: The word 'learning' is verbatim listed as a purpose, but the disambiguation rule reserves For Learning for learning the MODELING activity; here learning likely concerns the CM domain, which falls under Understanding.
